data.narrative:
LeydenJar Technologies is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Christian Rood and Gabriel de Scheemaker, having raised €23M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 6 same-stage, same-sector companies, supplemented by 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 additional private references, with top comparables including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable approach. The median valuation across the 6-company peer set is $355M, with a range spanning $120M (low) to $5.0B (high). Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B represents an outlier within the cohort; excluding extreme outliers, the tighter peer band clusters around $300M–$500M, represented by CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), Reverion ($310M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), and Pano AI ($500M).

The triangulation analysis, constrained by a single valuation method, implies a range of $302M (low) to $408M (high), with a central estimate of $355M. This range reflects the inherent dispersion within the peer universe rather than multiple independent methodologies. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of Commonwealth Fusion Systems as a true peer given its substantially higher valuation, and the lack of revenue or profitability metrics to calibrate early-stage positioning within the range. A more granular assignment toward the lower or upper bound would require additional context on LeydenJar's technology maturity, IP strength, and path to commercialization.
